blocks.spoken: Hi, um, welcome Alan, uh, to the to the meeting. Um, I've asked Alan to step in as acting Fullstack Manager for security policies, um, while I hire an EM. Um, Alan has graciously accepted, um, this this wonderful opportunity. So welcome Alan. Thank you.

blocks.spoken: Um, I've got the next item as well. Uh, so Anti-Abuse is moving product sections from SEC to Data Science. Um, Data Science is effectively a rename of the ModelOps, uh, section. So Data Science will include two stages, um, the existing ModelOps, which has three groups in it, which Mon can talk more about. And, um, uh, the Anti-Abuse stage, which only currently has one group, which is called Anti-Abuse. Uh, I think at some point there are plans to split Anti-Abuse into two groups, but that's not in the near near term. And, uh, we're working through all the changes around that. segment_summary: This segment of the meeting covers several organizational updates and discussions. Wayne Haber introduces the 'SEC' and 'Data Science' teams, noting a long meeting name and plans for more summarized communications. Phil Calder welcomes Alan as the acting Fullstack Manager for Security Policies and details the transition of Anti-Abuse to Data Science, which renames ModelOps. Thomas Woodham announces a second team within Dynamic Analysis, and Wayne Haber discusses a survey on improving the MR rate, which has been used to improve engineering velocity. Jay Swain and Neil McCarrison also share insights on how focusing on smaller, unscheduled tasks is positively impacting Merge Request rates.
